job_market_paper.title: Peacefully Demobilizing Rebels: Identity, Emotional Cues, and the FARC
job_market_paper.coauthors: with Michael Jetter and Christopher Parsons
job_market_paper.status: IZA Discussion Paper No. 16054; under journal review
job_market_paper.abstract: In the early 2000s, the Colombian government broadcast radio messages during national-team football matches encouraging FARC combatants to demobilize. We combine match dates and kick-off times with spatial and temporal variation in rain-induced radio reception in a municipality-day panel covering 2003-2016. Family-oriented messages induced more than 1,000 demobilizations, while appeals to national unity had no detectable effect. Persuasion was strongest following unexpected losses, when negative emotional cues made family identity more salient. A model combining identity salience and emotional state, together with tests using holidays and local climatic anomalies, supports this mechanism. The results show that the effectiveness of persuasion depends on the interaction between message content, identity, and emotional context.

working_papers.title: The Wrong Snowball: Reintegration Signals and Demobilization in Colombia
working_papers.summary: State-funded business openings by former combatants were followed by fewer nearby demobilizations, showing how visible but fragile reintegration outcomes can discourage armed-group exit.

working_papers.title: You Speak, We Shoot: On the Unintended Consequences of Community Radios on Colombia's Violent Conflict
working_papers.authors: with Gustavo Canavire-Bacarreza and Eric Neumayer
working_papers.summary: Community-radio entry increased killings by right-wing paramilitaries but not by the FARC, illustrating how participatory media can disrupt territorial power.

working_papers.title: Looks Expensive Until It Doesn't: The Timing of Pain-and-Palliative Care in Colombia
working_papers.summary: Using Colombia's national health claims and risk-set matching, earlier specialist access is associated with roughly 25 percent lower recorded consultation and procedure values than delayed access; a simple prior-spending rule captures most of the value of machine-learning targeting.

working_papers.title: The Reproducibility and Robustness of Economics and Political Science by Method and Field
working_papers.authors: with Abel Brodeur, Derek Mikola, Nikolai Cook, Lenka Fiala, Ghina Abdul Baki, et al.
working_papers.summary: Coordinates more than 1,000 researchers and 15,000 reanalyses of 248 economics and political-science studies to compare reproducibility and robustness across methods and fields.

working_papers.title: Mass Reproducibility in Psychological Science
working_papers.authors: with Abel Brodeur, Ghina Abdul Baki, Luna Fazio, and collaborators
working_papers.status: Manuscript in preparation; author list pending finalization (June 2026)
working_papers.summary: Reports a large-scale I4R-Psychological Science reproducibility initiative in which 63 independent groups document materials availability, coding and preregistration issues, and computational reproducibility.
